headline: Poo-pourri Park
subtext: Ratepayers fork out to clear dog excrement, as Council's 'Rubbish' management plan continues to amass public ire.
body:
Ward 1 Councillor, Cr T. Waffle, has declared a state of emergency for the Steyne's beloved B-Line footpath after an infestation of dog poo took over the park's grassy areas. The Council Chambers have been scrambling to devise methods to remove this stubborn material clogging up the area.

"Local Ratepayers are understandably outraged at the mess," Cr Waffle admitted during a press conference held in the shade of the Steyne's iconic water tower. "We're taking this issue seriously, and we've even consulted local pet owners to find ways to keep their dogs from doing their business near our beloved B-Line."

Despite these efforts, dog poo continues to accumulate at an alarming rate, posing a threat to public health and safety. The Remy Digest has chosen not to publish any images of the offending material due to its potential impact on delicate sensibilities.

The situation is so dire that park-goers are now advised to wear protective footwear when using the B-Line or risk getting their shoes encrusted in canine waste. "It's like walking through a minefield, but with dog poo instead," said one local resident who wished to remain anonymous.

The Council Chambers have put out an urgent call for public works' heavy machinery and professional cleaners to tackle this 21st-century plague sweeping across the Northern Beaches' green spaces. In the meantime, park-goers are advised to steer clear of the affected areas until further notice.

For now, our community is left grappling with the question: how did this happen, and can it be undone?

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.
